compiler_notes: AAA 2019-2024; AA+ 2025 (S&P methodology change). Standout: Danvers Electric ~$2,036/yr savings vs MA average.
Several inputs estimated (free cash %, GFOA years, debt/capita, crime ratios). Pull primary ACFR before external publication.

AA bond rating (S&P stable Oct 2024). Very low free cash 1.2% flagged. High flood trajectory (+12 pts by 2050). Pull debt/capita and tax_base split from ACFR. median_annual_tax_bill blank — pull from Census ACS.

PMLP municipal electric ~$1,850/yr savings vs MA avg. Bond rating and debt not found — pull from EMMA and ACFR. ACFR not published online (transparency=partial). Large commercial base (16.5% CIP share).
Bond rating estimated as AA — pull from EMMA (MSRB). Most financial metrics estimated; verify from ACFR. Tax base non-residential % estimated from Route 114 commercial concentration.

Bond rating estimated — pull from EMMA. Most metrics estimated; verify from ACFR and DESE. Flood risk reflects coastal exposure — pull First Street Foundation data. Debt/capita reflects recent capital projects.

Bond rating AA+ estimated — pull from EMMA. Very high commercial tax base (44.8%) keeps residential rates low. Major retail/office corridor Route 128/3. Most metrics estimated; verify from ACFR and DESE district profiles.
Bond rating not found — pull from EMMA (MSRB). Smaller rural-suburban town in Worcester County. High crime rates relative to Essex County peers. Many metrics estimated; verify from ACFR and DESE.

Chelsea Station (opened 2021) brings direct commuter rail to North Station in under 30 minutes, plus Silver Line SL3 to South Station — the strongest multi-modal transit access in the Civica cohort. A 26-year GFOA award signals consistent financial management despite significant demographic challenges.

Lexington's AAA bond rating and #4-ranked school district (MCAS math 78%; grad 98.2%) make it the highest-ranked school district in this group -- plan for one of MA's highest median tax bills at $14,714/yr.

transit_access: Bus only (Route 77 to Harvard Sq -- every 10-15 min)
compiler_notes: Arlington's Route 77 bus runs every 10-15 minutes to Harvard Square -- the most frequent transit link to Cambridge of any non-rail suburb -- and its school district climbed 8 spots to #24 statewide over the past decade.

Medford gained Green Line access via the GLX extension in 2022 -- one of the most significant transit upgrades to any MA suburb in a generation -- and median home values have not fully caught up to comparable Green Line cities yet.

transit_access: Commuter Rail (Fitchburg Line -- Belmont Center station)
compiler_notes: Belmont achieves top-tier school outcomes (#12 statewide; MCAS math 71%) at just $18,266 per pupil -- the lowest per-pupil spend of any top-15 district in this group -- while holding a Moody's Aaa bond rating.

transit_access: Commuter Rail (Lowell Line -- Winchester Center + Wedgemere stations)
compiler_notes: Winchester has the lowest violent crime rate of any town in this comparison group (22/100k) and a 95.7% residential tax base with minimal commercial subsidy -- yet still holds a Moody's Aaa rating and two Lowell Line commuter rail stops.

Woburn's biotech and industrial commercial tax base (est. ~35% of levy) gives residents a $8.54 residential rate -- among the lowest in Middlesex County -- and the Anderson/Woburn commuter rail stop provides direct access to North Station.

transit_access: Commuter Rail (Haverhill Line -- Wakefield + Wakefield Junction stations)
compiler_notes: Wakefield sits on scenic Lake Quannapowitt and has two Haverhill Line commuter rail stops -- a rare combination of waterfront quality and rail access at under $725K median home value.

Reading jumped 23 spots in the statewide school rankings -- the most dramatic improvement in this comparison group -- while maintaining the second-lowest violent crime rate (28/100k) and a 98.1% graduation rate.

transit_access: Subway (Green Line -- 5 GLX stations: Union Sq, E. Somerville, Gilman, Magoun, Ball Sq)
compiler_notes: Somerville added five Green Line stations via the GLX extension in 2022 and offers a 35% residential exemption -- reducing owner-occupant tax bills by ~$4,300/yr -- making the actual cost of ownership significantly lower than the headline rate suggests.

transit_access: Subway (Red Line -- Alewife, Porter, Harvard, Central, Kendall/MIT) + Fitchburg CR (Porter)
compiler_notes: Cambridge is the only Massachusetts municipality with a triple-AAA bond rating from all three major agencies (Moody's, S&P, Fitch) for 25 consecutive years -- enabled by Kendall Square's biotech and university commercial base, which also funds the lowest residential tax rate in Middlesex County.
~79% non-residential tax base subsidizes residential rates. BPS is a large urban district under state accountability.
Blue Line MBTA access. Coastal city with Revere Beach; significant flood exposure. Diverse and growing.
Peninsula nearly surrounded by water; very high flood risk. Small residential community with above-average schools.
Very high non-residential tax base (~72%) keeps residential rates among the lowest in the state. Green Line D branch + Commuter Rail. Top-10 school district.
Large commercial/biotech tax base along Route 128 corridor subsidizes residential rates. Fitchburg Line commuter rail.
Orange Line MBTA access. Diverse city with growing restaurant/retail scene. Schools below state average.
Encore Boston Harbor casino generates significant commercial tax revenue. Green Line Extension access. High flood risk near Mystic River.
Inner suburb with strong fiscal management and improving schools. Bus only — no rail in town. Charles River flood zone affects small portion.
Largest city in Middlesex County. Became a city in 2018. Strong commercial base along Route 9 and Mass Pike corridor. Worcester Line commuter rail.
Natick Mall retail corridor provides strong commercial tax base. Top-60 school district. Worcester Line commuter rail.
Acton-Boxborough Regional district is top-15 in the state. High residential tax rate offset by very high incomes and very low crime. Fitchburg Line commuter rail.
Concord-Carlisle Regional is top-15 in the state. Historic town with premium pricing. Concord River floodplain affects ~12% of land. Fitchburg Line commuter rail.
Solid suburban town with improving schools. Bus only — nearest MBTA rail at Malden Center (Orange Line). Generally good value relative to neighbors.
Split residential/commercial tax rate. Red Line MBTA: Quincy Center, Quincy Adams, North Quincy, JFK/UMass, Wollaston. Quincy Retirement System (~70% funded, own system). Tax rate estimated FY2025.
Red Line MBTA terminus (Braintree station). Major commercial base: South Shore Plaza. Norfolk County Retirement System (~69.5% funded). AAA bond rating.
AAA bond rating. Predominantly residential tax base (~13% non-res). Near Mattapan trolley border. School district ranked 37th of 351 statewide. Tax rate estimated FY2025.
Green Line B/C/D access throughout town. Brookline Retirement System (~76% funded, own system). AAA bond rating. 32+ consecutive GFOA certificates. Tax rate estimated FY2025.

Needham Branch commuter rail: Dedham Corporate Center, Endicott. Legacy Place retail complex. Some Charles River/Mother Brook flood exposure. School rank slipped ~15 spots past decade. Tax rate estimated FY2025.

AAA bond rating. Needham Branch commuter rail: Needham Heights, Needham Center, Needham Junction. Route 9 tech corridor / Needham Business Park. Free cash consistently 7-10%. Top 4% of MA school districts.

AAA bond rating. Framingham/Worcester Commuter Rail: Wellesley Farms, Wellesley Hills, Wellesley Square. Free cash ~13% — among highest in MA. 7th of 351 school districts statewide. Home of Wellesley College.

Old Colony/Greenbush Commuter Rail: East Weymouth, South Weymouth, Weymouth Landing/East Braintree. Coastal and tidal river flood exposure. School rank declined ~20 spots past decade. Tax rate estimated FY2025.
Lowest residential tax rate in Norfolk County (FY2025: $9.75). AAA bond rating. Providence/Stoughton Commuter Rail: Canton Center, Canton Junction. Strong commercial base along Route 138.

Providence Commuter Rail: Norwood Depot, Norwood Central. Very high non-residential tax base (~37%) — Route 1, University Ave industrial corridor. Free cash FY2026: 14.65%. School rank declined ~15 spots past decade.

AAA rated (S&P and Moody's Aaa confirmed Jan 2025). Greenbush commuter rail: West Hingham and Nantasket Junction stations. 28 consecutive GFOA certificates. Plymouth County Retirement Association (~65% funded, estimated). 13th of 351 school districts statewide.

AAA bond rating confirmed 2025. No commuter rail — nearest Greenbush stop is North Scituate (park-and-ride). Property crime 17/100k is among lowest in our dataset. Predominantly residential tax base (~5% non-res). Coastal flood exposure from barrier beach and multiple inlets.

AA+ bond rating confirmed. Greenbush commuter rail: North Scituate and Greenbush terminal stations. One of MA's most flood-prone coastal communities (~35% in FEMA hazard area, new maps added properties). Violent crime 20/100k among lowest in dataset.

Bond rating not publicly confirmed (small issuer, infrequent bonds). Greenbush commuter rail: Cohasset station. Top-5% school district (19th of 351). Very low crime. Coastal harbor flood exposure (~22%). Smallest town in dataset at 8,358 residents.

AAA bond rating maintained 15+ consecutive years (confirmed 2025). No commuter rail. Top 9% statewide schools (30th of 351) with improving trajectory. Very low crime. Inland — minimal flood risk. Route 53/Norwell Crossing commercial corridor.

Moody's Aa2 (AA equiv.) confirmed Feb 2025. No commuter rail — nearest Greenbush (Scituate) serves Hanover commuters via park-and-ride. Property crime 987/100k is inflated by Hanover Mall and Route 3/53 retail corridor, not residential crime. School district improving (52nd, up 10 spots).

FEMA confirmed: ~40% of buildings in Special Flood Hazard Area — one of the highest in MA. FEMA CRS Class 7 community. No commuter rail; Greenbush terminal (southern Scituate) is nearest access. Heavily residential tax base (~6% non-res). Lowest residential tax rate in Plymouth County dataset ($9.90).

Part of Silver Lake Regional school district (Kingston + Halifax + Plympton; ranked 168th of 351). Old Colony commuter rail (Kingston/Plymouth Line) active at Kingston station. Kingston Collection mall on Route 3 provides modest commercial tax base.

MBTA Plymouth station suspended indefinitely since April 5, 2021 — all Kingston/Plymouth Line trains terminate at Kingston. Bus only for Plymouth. School district ranked 187th, declining ~15 spots. Tourism economy (Plymouth Rock, Mayflower). Significant coastal and pond flood exposure.

Moody's A1 (A+ equiv.) confirmed. Own retirement system (Brockton Retirement System, ~57% funded, estimated). Old Colony commuter rail (Middleborough/Lakeville Line) and BAT local buses. School district 332/351 — bottom 6% statewide. Highest violent crime in our dataset (599/100k). Most affordable home prices in Plymouth County.
